dublinCore.dc:title: 116 S3275 IS: Humanoid Robotics Oversight and Blocking of Obtainment from Totalitarians Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cassidy (for himself and Mr. Coons) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: To reduce the risk to the national security of the United States posed by humanoid robots produced in certain countries, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Humanoid Robotics Oversight and Blocking of Obtainment from Totalitarians Act of 2025 or the Humanoid ROBOT Act of 2025.

paragraph.text: The term artificial intelligence has the meaning given that term in section 5002 of the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 (15 U.S.C. 9401).
The term country of concern has the meaning given the term covered nation in section 4872 of title 10, United States Code.

subparagraph.text: the government of a country of concern;
a political party of a country of concern;
a foreign entity of concern (as defined in section 9901 of the William M. (Mac) Thornberry National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2021 (15 U.S.C. 4651));

an entity identified by the Secretary of Defense under section 1260H(a) of the William M. (Mac) Thornberry National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2021 (Public Law 116–283; 10 U.S.C. 113 note) as a Chinese military company operating directly or indirectly in the United States;
an entity with its headquarters located in, organized under the laws of, or otherwise subject to the jurisdiction of, a country of concern; or
an entity owned or controlled by, or under common ownership or control with, an entity described in any of subparagraphs (A) through (E).

paragraph.text: The term executive agency has the meaning given that term in section 133 of title 41, United States Code.
The term humanoid robot means an autonomous or semi-autonomous machine that—

subparagraph.text: uses integrated artificial intelligence systems;
possesses a body structure that simulates the human form, including a head, torso, arms, and legs, or any configuration that resembles a human silhouette;
is capable of performing tasks associated with human activities; and
can communicate interactively, using natural language processing to understand verbal or written commands.

section.header: Prohibitions on Government contracting relating to humanoid robots from covered entities

subsection.header: Prohibition on purchases by executive agencies
subsection.text: The head of an executive agency may not enter into or renew a contract to procure or otherwise obtain a humanoid robot designed, tested, developed, or manufactured by a covered entity.

A person awarded a contract by an executive agency may not use a humanoid robot designed, tested, developed, or manufactured by a covered entity to fulfill, execute, or otherwise support carrying out the contract.

The Secretary of Defense may waive a prohibition under subsection (a) or (b) with respect to the purchase or use of a humanoid robot if using the robot is required for the completion of objectives related to national security or for research purposes.
The prohibitions under subsections (a) and (b) shall apply on and after the date that is180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act.

Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council shall amend the Federal Acquisition Regulation to implement the prohibitions under subsections (a) and (b), including by establishing a requirement for prime contractors to incorporate the substance of such prohibitions and applicable implementing contract clauses into contracts relating to humanoid robots.

section.header: Review by Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States of investments in production of humanoid robot technologies

subsection.text: Section 721(a)(4) of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C. 4565(a)(4)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in clause (i), by striking ; and and inserting a semicolon;
in clause (ii), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: any transaction described in subparagraph (B)(vi) proposed or pending on or after the date of the enactment of the Humanoid Robotics Oversight and Blocking of Obtainment from Totalitarians Act of 2025.

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (B), by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: Any other investment, subject to regulations prescribed under subparagraph (D)—

subclause.text: in a United States business that designs, tests, develops, or manufactures humanoid robots (as defined in section 2 of the Humanoid Robotics Oversight and Blocking of Obtainment from Totalitarians Act of 2025);

item.text: an entity organized under the laws of, or otherwise subject to the jurisdiction of, a covered nation (as defined in section 4872 of title 10, United States Code); or
an entity owned or controlled by, or under common ownership or control with, an entity described in item (aa); and

subclause.text: without regard to whether the investment results in control of the United States business by an entity described in subclause (II).

subparagraph.text: in clause (i), in the matter preceding subclause (I)—

clause.text: by striking subparagraph (B)(iii) and inserting clauses (iii) and (vi) of subparagraph (B); and
by striking that subparagraph and inserting that clause;

subparagraph.text: in clause (iii)(I), by striking subparagraph (B)(iii) and inserting clauses (iii) and (vi) of subparagraph (B); and
in clause (iv)(I), in the matter preceding item (aa)—

clause.text: by striking in subparagraph (B)(iii) and inserting in clause (iii) or (vi) of subparagraph (B); and
by striking of subparagraph (B)(iii) and inserting of such clauses.

subsection.text: Section 721(b)(1)(C)(v)(IV)(bb) of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C. 4565(b)(1)(C)(v)(IV)(bb)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subitem.text: The parties to a covered transaction described in subsection (a)(4)(B)(vi) shall submit a declaration described in subclause (I) with respect to the transaction.

section.header: Report on national security threats posed by humanoid robots

subsection.text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the committees specified in subsection (c) a detailed report on the threats to the national security of the United States posed by the development and use of humanoid robots for both civilian and military purposes in countries of concern.
The report required by subsection (a) shall include the following:

subparagraph.text: the economic infrastructure and manufacturing ecosystem for humanoid robots in the People's Republic of China and other countries of concern; and
military-civil fusion efforts, and other efforts, of the People’s Liberation Army relating to humanoid robots.

paragraph.text: An analysis of how the circumvention of United States export controls and the theft of intellectual property or proprietary information of United States persons has contributed or could contribute to the development of humanoid robots in countries of concern.
Recommendations to improve such export controls.
An analysis of the privacy and data security threats relating to the use of data of United States persons obtained through humanoid robots, including—

subparagraph.text: how the data might be stored, including whether the data is stored within the humanoid robot or on cloud infrastructure; and
how the data is accessed by the government of a country of concern.

paragraph.text: An analysis of the threat that data obtained through humanoid robots designed, tested, developed, or manufactured by covered entities poses with respect to economic espionage.

Recommendations for administrative and legislative action to address national security risks posed to the United States by humanoid robots designed, tested, developed, or manufactured by covered entities.
Any other information considered relevant by the Secretary.

subsection.text: The committees specified in this subsection are—

paragraph.text: the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation, the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs, the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate, and Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate; and
the Committee on Energy and Commerce, the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Armed Services, and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives.

subsection.text: The report required by subsection (a) shall be submitted in unclassified form but may include a classified annex.
